key_characteristics: Generated Django initial migration for menu module
Creates 8 models: Theme, Menu, Category, Product, ProductVariant, ProductModifier, Allergen, ProductAllergen, NutritionInfo
432 lines of migration code with comprehensive field definitions
Includes custom constraints (unique constraints, indexes) for optimal database performance
Implements audit trail fields (created_at, updated_at, deleted_at) with soft-delete support
Uses UUID primary keys across all models
Foreign key relationships properly configured with CASCADE delete strategies

model_structure.Theme: Menu styling with colors, fonts, CSS customization
model_structure.Menu: Restaurant menu container with publication status
model_structure.Product: Individual menu items with pricing and attributes

key_characteristics: Empty file to mark directory as Python package

patterns_discovered.description: All models include deleted_at field with null default and db_index for efficient soft-delete filtering
patterns_discovered.benefit: Maintains data integrity while enabling logical deletion

patterns_discovered.description: Standard created_at (auto_now_add) and updated_at (auto_now) timestamps on all models
patterns_discovered.benefit: Complete change history tracking without additional logging overhead

patterns_discovered.description: All models include ForeignKey to core.organization with CASCADE deletion
patterns_discovered.benefit: Ensures data isolation and organization-level access control

patterns_discovered.description: All nameable models (Theme, Menu, Category, Product) include slug fields with unique constraints
patterns_discovered.benefit: Human-readable URLs while maintaining referential integrity

patterns_discovered.description: Category model has optional self-referential parent field for nested categories
patterns_discovered.benefit: Supports unlimited category depth for complex menu structures

patterns_discovered.description: Product has both ProductVariant (size/portion) and ProductModifier (add-ons) relationships
patterns_discovered.benefit: Flexible product customization matching real restaurant menu needs

patterns_discovered.description: Multiple indexes on organization + field combinations and frequently queried columns
patterns_discovered.benefit: Optimized query performance for typical access patterns

patterns_discovered.description: settings and gallery fields use JSONField for flexible configuration

gotchas_discovered.description: Category slug is unique per menu, not globally. Product slug is unique per category. This requires careful query construction to avoid assumptions.
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Always filter by menu/category when querying by slug at that level

gotchas_discovered.description: Multiple CASCADE relationships (Category->Menu, Product->Category, ProductVariant->Product, etc.) mean deleting a Menu will cascade through entire hierarchy
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Use soft deletes (deleted_at) or consider ON_DELETE=PROTECT for critical relationships

gotchas_discovered.description: ProductAllergen includes quantity_mg field suggesting allergen concentration, but Allergen model lacks units/thresholds for safe consumption levels
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Consider adding allergen severity levels and safe threshold fields to Allergen model

gotchas_discovered.description: NutritionInfo is one-to-one to Product but field is nullable, creating orphaned nutrition records if Product is deleted
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Consider CASCADE deletion or making relationship required if nutrition data is always needed

gotchas_discovered.description: Theme is set at Menu level but Theme model has organization scope, creating potential permission mismatches
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Add validation to ensure Theme belongs to same Organization as Menu

approach_outcome.summary: Successfully generated comprehensive Django initial migration for menu module with 9 models and proper database constraints
key_accomplishments: Created production-ready migration with proper field types and constraints
Implemented multi-tenancy through organization foreign keys
Designed hierarchical product categorization with soft-delete support
Added comprehensive indexing for query optimization
Established audit trail with timestamps on all models
Created proper junction tables for many-to-many relationships (ProductAllergen)
quality_indicators: All help_text fields populated for documentation
Consistent naming conventions (snake_case for fields, CamelCase for models)
Proper verbose_name and verbose_name_plural for admin interface
Default ordering specified for all models

recommendations.recommendation: Add database-level check constraints for decimal fields (base_price must be >= 0, quantity_mg must be >= 0)
recommendations.rationale: Prevents invalid data at database level rather than application level

recommendations.recommendation: Verify that organization foreign keys are properly enforced in querysets and serializers to prevent cross-tenant data access
Create test migrations that verify: (1) forward and reverse migrations work, (2) constraints are properly enforced, (3) indexes are created
Consider adding Chef/Admin notes field to Product for kitchen-specific information not shown to customers
Add allergen_severity and safe_threshold fields to Allergen model for better health/safety tracking
recommendations.rationale: Supports regulatory compliance and customer safety

recommendations.recommendation: Consider adding select_related/prefetch_related hints in model docstrings or custom managers for common query patterns
recommendations.rationale: Helps developers avoid N+1 query problems

recommendations.recommendation: Document the JSON fields (settings, gallery) with example schemas in model docstrings
recommendations.rationale: Helps future developers understand expected structure
